_meta.description: Baltimore Catechism Q&A pairs (public domain, 1885 edition)

questions.question: Who made us?
questions.answer: God made us.

questions.question: Who is God?
questions.answer: God is the Supreme Being, infinitely perfect, who made all things and keeps them in existence.

questions.question: Why did God make us?
questions.answer: God made us to show forth His goodness and to share with us His everlasting happiness in heaven.

questions.question: What must we do to gain the happiness of heaven?
questions.answer: To gain the happiness of heaven we must know, love, and serve God in this world.

questions.question: From whom do we learn to know, love, and serve God?
questions.answer: We learn to know, love, and serve God from Jesus Christ, the Son of God, who teaches us through the Catholic Church.

questions.question: Where do we find the chief truths taught by Jesus Christ through the Catholic Church?
questions.answer: We find the chief truths taught by Jesus Christ through the Catholic Church in the Apostles' Creed.

questions.question: Say the Apostles' Creed.
questions.answer: I believe in God, the Father Almighty, Creator of heaven and earth; and in Jesus Christ, His only Son, our Lord; who was conceived by the Holy Ghost, born of the Virgin Mary, suffered under Pontius Pilate, was crucified, died, and was buried. He descended into hell; the third day He arose again from the dead; He ascended into heaven, sitteth at the right hand of God, the Father Almighty; from thence He shall come to judge the living and the dead. I believe in the Holy Ghost, the holy Catholic Church, the communion of saints, the forgiveness of sins, the resurrection of the body, and life everlasting. Amen.

questions.question: What is God?
questions.answer: God is a spirit infinitely perfect.

questions.question: Had God a beginning?
questions.answer: God had no beginning; He always was and He always will be.

questions.question: Where is God?
questions.answer: God is everywhere.

questions.question: Does God see us?
questions.answer: God sees us and watches over us.

questions.question: Does God know all things?
questions.answer: God knows all things, even our most secret thoughts, words, and actions.

questions.question: Can God do all things?
questions.answer: God can do all things, and nothing is hard or impossible to Him.

questions.question: Is God just, holy, and merciful?
questions.answer: God is all just, all holy, all merciful, as He is infinitely perfect.

questions.question: Is there but one God?
questions.answer: Yes; there is but one God.

questions.question: How many Persons are there in God?
questions.answer: In God there are three Divine Persons — the Father, the Son, and the Holy Ghost.

questions.question: Is the Father God?
questions.answer: The Father is God and the first Person of the Blessed Trinity.

questions.question: Is the Son God?
questions.answer: The Son is God and the second Person of the Blessed Trinity.

questions.question: Is the Holy Ghost God?
questions.answer: The Holy Ghost is God and the third Person of the Blessed Trinity.

questions.question: What do you mean by the Blessed Trinity?
questions.answer: By the Blessed Trinity I mean one God in three Divine Persons.

questions.question: Are the three Divine Persons equal in all things?
questions.answer: The three Divine Persons are equal in all things.

questions.question: Are the three Divine Persons one and the same God?
questions.answer: The three Divine Persons are one and the same God, having one and the same divine nature and substance.

questions.question: Can we fully understand how the three Divine Persons are one and the same God?
questions.answer: We cannot fully understand how the three Divine Persons are one and the same God, because this is a mystery.

questions.question: What is a mystery?
questions.answer: A mystery is a truth which we cannot fully understand, but which we firmly believe because God has revealed it.

questions.question: Who created heaven and earth, and all things?
questions.answer: God created heaven and earth, and all things.

questions.question: What are angels?
questions.answer: Angels are pure spirits without a body, created to adore and enjoy God in heaven.

questions.question: Were the angels created for any other purpose?
questions.answer: The angels were also created to assist before the throne of God and to minister to Him; they have often been sent as messengers from God to man; and are also appointed our guardians.

questions.question: Were all the angels faithful to God?
questions.answer: Not all the angels were faithful to God; some of them sinned by pride and were cast into hell, and these are called devils or bad angels.

questions.question: Who were the first man and woman?
questions.answer: The first man and woman were Adam and Eve.

questions.question: Were Adam and Eve innocent and holy when they came from the hand of God?
questions.answer: Adam and Eve were innocent and holy when they came from the hand of God.

questions.question: Did Adam and Eve remain faithful to God?
questions.answer: Adam and Eve did not remain faithful to God, but broke His command by eating the forbidden fruit.

questions.question: What befell Adam and Eve on account of their sin?
questions.answer: Adam and Eve, on account of their sin, lost innocence and holiness, and were doomed to sickness and death.

questions.question: What is the sin called which we inherit from our first parents?
questions.answer: The sin which we inherit from our first parents is called original sin.

questions.question: Was any human person ever preserved from original sin?
questions.answer: The Blessed Virgin Mary, through the merits of her Divine Son, was preserved free from the guilt of original sin, and this privilege is called her Immaculate Conception.

questions.question: What is actual sin?
questions.answer: Actual sin is any willful thought, word, deed, or omission contrary to the law of God.

questions.question: How many kinds of actual sin are there?
questions.answer: There are two kinds of actual sin — mortal sin and venial sin.

questions.question: What is mortal sin?
questions.answer: Mortal sin is a grievous offense against the law of God.

questions.question: Why is this sin called mortal?
questions.answer: This sin is called mortal because it deprives us of spiritual life, which is sanctifying grace, and brings everlasting death and damnation on the soul.

questions.question: How many things are necessary to make a sin mortal?
questions.answer: To make a sin mortal three things are necessary: a grievous matter, sufficient reflection, and full consent of the will.

questions.question: What is venial sin?
questions.answer: Venial sin is a slight offense against the law of God in matters of less importance, or in matters of great importance it is an offense committed without sufficient reflection or full consent of the will.

questions.question: Which are the effects of venial sin?
questions.answer: The effects of venial sin are the lessening of the love of God in our heart, the making us less worthy of His help, and the weakening of our power to resist mortal sin.

questions.question: Which are the chief sources of sin?
questions.answer: The chief sources of sin are seven: Pride, Covetousness, Lust, Anger, Gluttony, Envy, and Sloth, and they are commonly called the seven capital sins.

questions.question: Did God abandon man after he fell into sin?
questions.answer: God did not abandon man after he fell into sin, but promised him a Redeemer, who was to satisfy for man's sin and reopen to him the gates of heaven.

questions.question: Who is the Redeemer?
questions.answer: Our Blessed Lord and Savior Jesus Christ is the Redeemer of mankind.

questions.question: What do you believe of Jesus Christ?
questions.answer: I believe that Jesus Christ is the Son of God, the second Person of the Blessed Trinity, true God and true man.

questions.question: Why is Jesus Christ true God?
questions.answer: Jesus Christ is true God because He is the true and only Son of God the Father.

questions.question: Why is Jesus Christ true man?
questions.answer: Jesus Christ is true man because He is the Son of the Blessed Virgin Mary and has a body and soul like ours.

questions.question: How many natures are there in Jesus Christ?
questions.answer: In Jesus Christ there are two natures: the nature of God and the nature of man.

questions.question: Is Jesus Christ more than one person?
questions.answer: No, Jesus Christ is but one Divine Person.

questions.question: Was Jesus Christ always God?
questions.answer: Jesus Christ was always God, as He is the second Person of the Blessed Trinity, equal to His Father from all eternity.

questions.question: Was Jesus Christ always man?
questions.answer: Jesus Christ was not always man, but became man at the time of His Incarnation.

questions.question: What do you mean by the Incarnation?
questions.answer: By the Incarnation I mean that the Son of God was made man.

questions.question: How was the Son of God made man?
questions.answer: The Son of God was conceived and made man by the power of the Holy Ghost, in the womb of the Blessed Virgin Mary.

questions.question: Is the Blessed Virgin Mary truly the Mother of God?
questions.answer: The Blessed Virgin Mary is truly the Mother of God, because the same Divine Person who is the Son of God is also the Son of the Blessed Virgin Mary.

questions.question: Did the Son of God become man immediately after the sin of our first parents?
questions.answer: The Son of God did not become man immediately after the sin of our first parents, but was promised to them as a Redeemer.

questions.question: How long was the Son of God on earth?
questions.answer: The Son of God was on earth about thirty-three years.

questions.question: What did Jesus Christ do during the time He was on earth?
questions.answer: During the time He was on earth, Jesus Christ taught His divine doctrine, wrought miracles, and gave us the example of a most holy life.

questions.question: What sufferings did Jesus Christ endure for us?
questions.answer: Jesus Christ suffered a bloody sweat, a cruel scourging, was crowned with thorns, and was crucified.

questions.question: On what day did Christ die?
questions.answer: Christ died on Good Friday.

questions.question: Why did Jesus Christ suffer and die?
questions.answer: Jesus Christ suffered and died for our sins.

questions.question: What do we mean by the Resurrection?
questions.answer: By the Resurrection we mean that Jesus Christ rose from the dead, on the third day after His death.

questions.question: On what day did Christ rise from the dead?
questions.answer: Christ rose from the dead, glorious and immortal, on Easter Sunday, the third day after His death.

questions.question: How long did Christ stay on earth after His Resurrection?
questions.answer: Christ stayed on earth forty days after His Resurrection to show that He was truly risen from the dead and to instruct His Apostles.

questions.question: After Christ had remained forty days on earth, whither did He go?
questions.answer: After forty days Christ ascended into heaven, and the day on which He ascended into heaven is called Ascension Thursday.

questions.question: Where is Jesus Christ in heaven?
questions.answer: In heaven Jesus Christ sits at the right hand of God the Father Almighty.

questions.question: When will Christ come again?
questions.answer: Christ will come again from heaven at the last day to judge the living and the dead.

questions.question: What do you mean by the Holy Ghost?
questions.answer: By the Holy Ghost I mean the third Person of the Blessed Trinity.

questions.question: On what day did the Holy Ghost come down upon the Apostles?
questions.answer: The Holy Ghost came down upon the Apostles on Whitsunday or Pentecost Sunday, ten days after the Ascension of our Lord; and the day on which the Holy Ghost came down upon the Apostles is called Whitsunday, or Pentecost.

questions.question: What did the Holy Ghost do for the Apostles?
questions.answer: The Holy Ghost, descending upon the Apostles, enlightened their minds, strengthened their wills, and made them zealous in preaching the Gospel.

questions.question: Will the Holy Ghost abide with the Church forever?
questions.answer: The Holy Ghost will abide with the Church forever, and guide her in all truth and holiness.

questions.question: What is the Church?
questions.answer: The Church is the congregation of all baptized persons united in the same true faith, the same sacrifice, and the same sacraments, under the authority of the Sovereign Pontiff and the bishops in communion with him.

questions.question: Who founded the Church?
questions.answer: Jesus Christ founded the Church.

questions.question: Why did Jesus Christ found the Church?
questions.answer: Jesus Christ founded the Church to teach, govern, sanctify, and save all men.

questions.question: Are all bound to belong to the Church?
questions.answer: All are bound to belong to the Church, and he who knows the Church to be the true Church and remains out of it, cannot be saved.

questions.question: Which are the chief attributes of the Church?
questions.answer: The chief attributes of the Church are authority, infallibility, and indefectibility.

questions.question: What do you mean by the authority of the Church?
questions.answer: By the authority of the Church I mean the right and power which the Pope and the bishops, as the successors of the Apostles, have to teach and to govern the faithful.

questions.question: What do you mean by the infallibility of the Church?
questions.answer: By the infallibility of the Church I mean that the Church cannot err when it teaches a doctrine of faith or morals.

questions.question: What do you mean by the indefectibility of the Church?
questions.answer: By the indefectibility of the Church I mean that the Church, as Christ founded it, will last till the end of time.

questions.question: Which are the marks of the Church?
questions.answer: The marks of the Church are four: It is One, Holy, Catholic, and Apostolic.

questions.question: How is the Church One?
questions.answer: The Church is One because all its members agree in one faith, are all in one communion, and are all under one head.

questions.question: How is the Church Holy?
questions.answer: The Church is Holy because its founder, Jesus Christ, is holy; because it teaches a holy doctrine; invites all to a holy life; and because of the eminent holiness of so many thousands of its children.

questions.question: How is the Church Catholic or universal?
questions.answer: The Church is Catholic or universal because it subsists in all ages, teaches all nations, and maintains all truth.

questions.question: How is the Church Apostolic?
questions.answer: The Church is Apostolic because it was founded by Christ on His Apostles, and is governed by their lawful successors, and because it has never ceased, and never will cease, to teach their doctrine.

questions.question: Who is the visible head of the Church?
questions.answer: The visible head of the Church is the Pope, the Bishop of Rome, who is the Vicar of Christ on earth.

questions.question: Who is the invisible head of the Church?
questions.answer: The invisible head of the Church is Jesus Christ.

questions.question: What is the communion of saints?
questions.answer: The communion of saints is the union of the faithful on earth, the blessed in heaven, and the souls in purgatory, with Christ as their head.

questions.question: What is meant by the forgiveness of sins?
questions.answer: By the forgiveness of sins is meant that God has left the power of forgiving sins with the Church.

questions.question: What do you mean by the resurrection of the body?
questions.answer: By the resurrection of the body I mean that at the last day the bodies of all men will rise from the earth and be united again to their souls, never more to be separated.

questions.question: What do you mean by life everlasting?
questions.answer: By life everlasting I mean that the good shall live forever in the happiness of heaven.

questions.question: What is a sacrament?
questions.answer: A sacrament is an outward sign instituted by Christ to give grace.

questions.question: How many sacraments are there?
questions.answer: There are seven sacraments: Baptism, Confirmation, Holy Eucharist, Penance, Anointing of the Sick, Holy Orders, and Matrimony.

questions.question: What is Baptism?
questions.answer: Baptism is the sacrament which cleanses us from original sin, makes us Christians, children of God, and heirs of heaven.

questions.question: Is Baptism necessary for salvation?
questions.answer: Baptism is necessary for salvation, because without it we cannot enter into the kingdom of heaven.

questions.question: Who can administer Baptism?
questions.answer: A priest is the ordinary minister of Baptism; but in case of necessity, anyone who has the use of reason may baptize.

questions.question: How is Baptism given?
questions.answer: Baptism is given by pouring water on the head of the person to be baptized, saying at the same time, 'I baptize thee in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost.'

questions.question: What is Confirmation?
questions.answer: Confirmation is a sacrament through which we receive the Holy Ghost to make us strong and perfect Christians and soldiers of Jesus Christ.

questions.question: Who is the ordinary minister of Confirmation?
questions.answer: The bishop is the ordinary minister of Confirmation.

questions.question: What are the effects of Confirmation?
questions.answer: The effects of Confirmation are an increase of sanctifying grace, the strengthening of our faith, and the gifts of the Holy Ghost.

questions.question: What are the gifts of the Holy Ghost?
questions.answer: The gifts of the Holy Ghost are Wisdom, Understanding, Counsel, Fortitude, Knowledge, Piety, and Fear of the Lord.

questions.question: What is the Holy Eucharist?
questions.answer: The Holy Eucharist is the sacrament which contains the body and blood, soul and divinity, of our Lord Jesus Christ under the appearances of bread and wine.

questions.question: When did Christ institute the Holy Eucharist?
questions.answer: Christ instituted the Holy Eucharist at the Last Supper, the night before He died.

questions.question: Who can change bread and wine into the body and blood of Christ?
questions.answer: The priest alone can change bread and wine into the body and blood of Christ.

questions.question: How does the priest change the bread and wine into the body and blood of Christ?
questions.answer: The priest changes the bread and wine into the body and blood of Christ through the words of consecration in the Mass.

questions.question: What is this change of the bread and wine into the body and blood of Christ called?
questions.answer: This change of the bread and wine into the body and blood of Christ is called Transubstantiation.

questions.question: Is Jesus Christ whole and entire both under the form of bread and under the form of wine?
questions.answer: Jesus Christ is whole and entire both under the form of bread and under the form of wine.

questions.question: What is the Mass?
questions.answer: The Mass is the unbloody sacrifice of the body and blood of Christ.

questions.question: Is the Mass the same sacrifice as that of the Cross?
questions.answer: The Mass is the same sacrifice as that of the Cross.

questions.question: What are the purposes for which the Mass is offered?
questions.answer: The purposes for which the Mass is offered are: first, to adore God as our Creator; second, to thank God for His blessings; third, to ask God's blessings on all men; fourth, to satisfy the justice of God for the sins committed against Him.

questions.question: What is Holy Communion?
questions.answer: Holy Communion is the receiving of the body and blood of Christ.

questions.question: What is necessary to receive Holy Communion worthily?
questions.answer: To receive Holy Communion worthily it is necessary to be in the state of grace and to observe the prescribed fast.

questions.question: Does he who receives Communion in mortal sin receive the body and blood of Christ?
questions.answer: He who receives Communion in mortal sin receives the body and blood of Christ, but does not receive His grace, and commits a great sacrilege.

questions.question: What is the sacrament of Penance?
questions.answer: Penance is the sacrament by which the sins committed after Baptism are forgiven.

questions.question: What must we do to receive the sacrament of Penance worthily?
questions.answer: To receive the sacrament of Penance worthily we must: first, examine our conscience; second, have sorrow for our sins; third, make a firm resolution never more to offend God; fourth, confess our sins to the priest; fifth, accept the penance which the priest gives us.

questions.question: What is contrition?
questions.answer: Contrition is sincere sorrow for having offended God, and hatred of sin with a firm purpose of sinning no more.

questions.question: What is perfect contrition?
questions.answer: Perfect contrition is sorrow for sin arising from the love of God, whom we have offended because He is infinitely good in Himself and worthy of all love.

questions.question: What is imperfect contrition?
questions.answer: Imperfect contrition is sorrow for sin arising from any motive other than the perfect love of God, such as from the fear of hell or the loss of heaven.

questions.question: What is confession?
questions.answer: Confession is the telling of our sins to a duly authorized priest, for the purpose of obtaining forgiveness.

questions.question: What sins are we bound to confess?
questions.answer: We are bound to confess all our mortal sins, but it is well also to confess our venial sins.

questions.question: What is the sacrament of Anointing of the Sick?
questions.answer: Anointing of the Sick is a sacrament instituted to give spiritual aid and comfort and perfect spiritual health, including, if need be, the remission of sins, and also, if God wills, to restore bodily health, to those who are in danger of death from sickness, accident, or old age.

questions.question: Who can receive the Anointing of the Sick?
questions.answer: Any Catholic who has reached the use of reason and is in danger of death from sickness, accident, or old age can and should receive the Anointing of the Sick.

questions.question: What is the sacrament of Holy Orders?
questions.answer: Holy Orders is the sacrament by which bishops, priests, and other ministers of the Church are ordained and receive the power and grace to perform their sacred duties.

questions.question: What is the sacrament of Matrimony?
questions.answer: Matrimony is the sacrament which unites a Christian man and woman in lawful marriage.

questions.question: Can a Christian man and woman be united in lawful marriage in any other way than by the sacrament of Matrimony?
questions.answer: A Christian man and woman cannot be united in lawful marriage in any other way than by the sacrament of Matrimony, because Christ raised marriage to the dignity of a sacrament.

questions.question: Can the bond of Christian marriage be dissolved by any human power?
questions.answer: The bond of Christian marriage cannot be dissolved by any human power.

questions.question: What are the chief duties of husband and wife?
questions.answer: The chief duties of husband and wife are to be faithful to each other, to provide for the spiritual and temporal welfare of their children, and to give good example to all.

questions.question: What is grace?
questions.answer: Grace is a supernatural gift of God bestowed on us through the merits of Jesus Christ for our salvation.

questions.question: How many kinds of grace are there?
questions.answer: There are two kinds of grace: sanctifying grace and actual grace.

questions.question: What is sanctifying grace?
questions.answer: Sanctifying grace is that grace which makes the soul holy and pleasing to God.

questions.question: What is actual grace?
questions.answer: Actual grace is a supernatural help of God which enlightens our mind and strengthens our will to do good and to avoid evil.

questions.question: Is grace necessary for salvation?
questions.answer: Grace is necessary for salvation, because without grace we can do nothing to merit heaven.

questions.question: What are the Commandments of God?
questions.answer: The Commandments of God are the ten moral laws which God gave to Moses on Mount Sinai.

questions.question: Repeat the Ten Commandments of God.
questions.answer: 1. I am the Lord thy God; thou shalt not have strange gods before Me. 2. Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord thy God in vain. 3. Remember thou keep holy the Lord's Day. 4. Honor thy father and thy mother. 5. Thou shalt not kill. 6. Thou shalt not commit adultery. 7. Thou shalt not steal. 8. Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbor. 9. Thou shalt not covet thy neighbor's wife. 10. Thou shalt not covet thy neighbor's goods.

questions.question: What is the first Commandment?
questions.answer: The first Commandment is: I am the Lord thy God; thou shalt not have strange gods before Me.

questions.question: What does the first Commandment command?
questions.answer: The first Commandment commands us to worship the one, true God by faith, hope, charity, and religion.

questions.question: How do we sin against faith?
questions.answer: We sin against faith by not trying to know what God has taught, by refusing to believe all that God has taught, and by neglecting to profess our belief in what God has taught.

questions.question: How does a Catholic sin against faith?
questions.answer: A Catholic sins against faith by apostasy, heresy, indifferentism, and by taking part in non-Catholic worship.

questions.question: What does the first Commandment forbid?
questions.answer: The first Commandment forbids all false worship, the giving to a creature the honor which belongs to God alone, and also all dealing with the devil and superstition.

questions.question: Does the first Commandment forbid the honoring of the saints?
questions.answer: The first Commandment does not forbid the honoring of the saints, but rather approves of it; because by honoring the saints, who are the chosen friends of God, we honor God Himself.

questions.question: Does the first Commandment forbid the making of images?
questions.answer: The first Commandment does not forbid the making of images, but the making of idols; that is, it forbids us to make or worship anything as God, or in place of God.

questions.question: What is the second Commandment?
questions.answer: The second Commandment is: Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord thy God in vain.

questions.question: What does the second Commandment forbid?
questions.answer: The second Commandment forbids all false, rash, unjust, and unnecessary oaths, blasphemy, cursing, and profane use of God's name.

questions.question: What is the third Commandment?
questions.answer: The third Commandment is: Remember thou keep holy the Lord's Day.

questions.question: What does the third Commandment command?
questions.answer: The third Commandment commands us to sanctify Sunday as the Lord's Day.

questions.question: How are we to keep Sunday holy?
questions.answer: We are to keep Sunday holy by hearing Mass, by prayer and good works, and by rest from servile work.

questions.question: Are we bound to hear Mass on Sundays and holy days of obligation?
questions.answer: We are bound to hear Mass on Sundays and holy days of obligation, under pain of mortal sin, unless prevented by some serious reason.

questions.question: What is the fourth Commandment?
questions.answer: The fourth Commandment is: Honor thy father and thy mother.

questions.question: What does the fourth Commandment command?
questions.answer: The fourth Commandment commands us to respect and love our parents, to obey them in all that is not sinful, and to help them in their needs.

questions.question: Does the fourth Commandment oblige us to respect and obey others besides our parents?
questions.answer: The fourth Commandment obliges us to respect and obey, besides our parents, all our lawful superiors, both spiritual and temporal.

questions.question: What are the duties of parents toward their children?
questions.answer: The duties of parents toward their children are to provide for their bodily and spiritual welfare, to give them a Christian education, and to give them good example.

questions.question: What is the fifth Commandment?
questions.answer: The fifth Commandment is: Thou shalt not kill.

questions.question: What does the fifth Commandment forbid?
questions.answer: The fifth Commandment forbids all willful murder, fighting, anger, hatred, revenge, and bad example.

questions.question: Does the fifth Commandment forbid anger?
questions.answer: The fifth Commandment forbids unjust anger, and still more, hatred and revenge.

questions.question: Is it ever lawful to kill in self-defense?
questions.answer: It is lawful to kill in self-defense when we are unjustly attacked and have no other means of saving our own lives.

questions.question: Is suicide a mortal sin?
questions.answer: Suicide, or the intentional killing of one's self, is a mortal sin, because God alone is the master of our life, and it is for Him to decide when it shall end.

questions.question: What is the sixth Commandment?
questions.answer: The sixth Commandment is: Thou shalt not commit adultery.

questions.question: What does the sixth Commandment forbid?
questions.answer: The sixth Commandment forbids all impurity and immodesty in words, looks, and actions, whether alone or with others.

questions.question: What are the chief means of preserving the virtue of chastity?
questions.answer: The chief means of preserving the virtue of chastity are to guard the senses, especially the eyes, to practice self-denial and mortification, to avoid occasions of sin, and to frequent the sacraments of Penance and the Holy Eucharist, and to have a tender devotion to the Blessed Virgin.

questions.question: What is the seventh Commandment?
questions.answer: The seventh Commandment is: Thou shalt not steal.

questions.question: What does the seventh Commandment forbid?
questions.answer: The seventh Commandment forbids all unjust taking or keeping of what belongs to another.

questions.question: Are we bound to restore ill-gotten goods?
questions.answer: We are bound to restore ill-gotten goods, or their value, as far as we are able; otherwise we cannot be forgiven.

questions.question: Are we obliged to repair the damage we have unjustly caused?
questions.answer: We are bound to repair the damage we have unjustly caused to the property or reputation of another, as far as we are able.

questions.question: What is the eighth Commandment?
questions.answer: The eighth Commandment is: Thou shalt not bear false witness against thy neighbor.

questions.question: What does the eighth Commandment forbid?
questions.answer: The eighth Commandment forbids all rash judgment, backbiting, slander, and lies.

questions.question: What must they do who have lied about their neighbor and seriously injured his character?
questions.answer: They who have lied about their neighbor and seriously injured his character must repair the injury done as far as they are able, otherwise they will not be forgiven.

questions.question: What is the ninth Commandment?
questions.answer: The ninth Commandment is: Thou shalt not covet thy neighbor's wife.

questions.question: What does the ninth Commandment forbid?
questions.answer: The ninth Commandment forbids all thoughts and desires contrary to the virtue of purity.

questions.question: What is the tenth Commandment?
questions.answer: The tenth Commandment is: Thou shalt not covet thy neighbor's goods.

questions.question: What does the tenth Commandment forbid?
questions.answer: The tenth Commandment forbids all desire to take or keep unjustly what belongs to another.

questions.question: What are the chief commandments, or laws, of the Church?
questions.answer: The chief commandments of the Church are six: 1. To hear Mass on Sundays and holy days of obligation. 2. To fast and abstain on the days appointed. 3. To confess at least once a year. 4. To receive Holy Communion during the Easter time. 5. To contribute to the support of the Church. 6. To observe the laws of the Church concerning marriage.

questions.question: What is the obligation imposed by the first commandment of the Church?
questions.answer: The first commandment of the Church obliges us, under pain of mortal sin, to hear Mass on all Sundays and holy days of obligation.

questions.question: What is the obligation imposed by the second commandment of the Church?
questions.answer: The second commandment of the Church obliges us to fast and to abstain from meat on the days appointed by the Church.

questions.question: What is the obligation imposed by the third commandment of the Church?
questions.answer: The third commandment of the Church obliges us to confess our sins at least once a year.

questions.question: What is the obligation imposed by the fourth commandment of the Church?
questions.answer: The fourth commandment of the Church obliges us to receive Holy Communion during the Easter time.

questions.question: What is the obligation imposed by the fifth commandment of the Church?
questions.answer: The fifth commandment of the Church obliges us to contribute to the support of the Church.

questions.question: What is a virtue?
questions.answer: A virtue is a habit which disposes us to do good.

questions.question: What are the three theological virtues?
questions.answer: The three theological virtues are Faith, Hope, and Charity.

questions.question: What is faith?
questions.answer: Faith is the virtue by which we firmly believe all the truths God has revealed, on the word of God revealing them, who can neither deceive nor be deceived.

questions.question: What is hope?
questions.answer: Hope is the virtue by which we firmly trust that God will give us eternal life and the means to obtain it.

questions.question: What is charity?
questions.answer: Charity is the virtue by which we love God above all things for His own sake, and our neighbor as ourselves for the love of God.

questions.question: Which are the four cardinal virtues?
questions.answer: The four cardinal virtues are Prudence, Justice, Fortitude, and Temperance.

questions.question: What is prayer?
questions.answer: Prayer is the lifting up of our minds and hearts to God to adore Him, to thank Him for His benefits, to ask His forgiveness, and to beg of Him all the graces we need whether for soul or body.

questions.question: Is prayer necessary for salvation?
questions.answer: Prayer is necessary for salvation, and without it no one having the use of reason can be saved.

questions.question: At what times in particular should we pray?
questions.answer: We should pray in particular in all dangers, temptations, and afflictions; in the morning and at night; before and after meals; and above all on Sundays and holy days.

questions.question: How should we pray?
questions.answer: We should pray with attention, with a sense of our own helplessness, with a great desire for the graces we beg of God, with trust in God's goodness, and with perseverance.

questions.question: Which is the best of all prayers?
questions.answer: The best of all prayers is the Our Father, or the Lord's Prayer.

questions.question: Say the Our Father.
questions.answer: Our Father, who art in heaven, hallowed be Thy name; Thy kingdom come; Thy will be done on earth as it is in heaven. Give us this day our daily bread; and forgive us our trespasses, as we forgive those who trespass against us; and lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from evil. Amen.

questions.question: Who composed the Our Father?
questions.answer: Jesus Christ Himself composed the Our Father.

questions.question: Why is the Our Father the best of all prayers?
questions.answer: The Our Father is the best of all prayers because it is the Lord's Prayer, taught us by Jesus Christ Himself, and is a prayer of body and soul.

questions.question: How many petitions are in the Our Father?
questions.answer: In the Our Father there are seven petitions.

questions.question: Say the Hail Mary.
questions.answer: Hail Mary, full of grace, the Lord is with thee; blessed art thou among women, and blessed is the fruit of thy womb, Jesus. Holy Mary, Mother of God, pray for us sinners, now and at the hour of our death. Amen.

questions.question: Why should we have a great devotion to the Blessed Virgin?
questions.answer: We should have a great devotion to the Blessed Virgin because she is the Mother of God, and our most loving Mother.

questions.question: What is the Rosary?
questions.answer: The Rosary is a form of prayer in which we meditate on the chief mysteries of our redemption and recite fifteen decades of Hail Marys with an Our Father before each decade.

questions.question: What is an indulgence?
questions.answer: An indulgence is the remission in whole or in part of the temporal punishment due to sin.

questions.question: How many kinds of indulgences are there?
questions.answer: There are two kinds of indulgences — plenary and partial.

questions.question: What is a plenary indulgence?
questions.answer: A plenary indulgence is the full remission of the temporal punishment due to sin.

questions.question: What is a partial indulgence?
questions.answer: A partial indulgence is the remission of part of the temporal punishment due to sin.

questions.question: What are the last things to be ever remembered?
questions.answer: The four last things to be ever remembered are Death, Judgment, Heaven, and Hell.

questions.question: What is death?
questions.answer: Death is the separation of the soul from the body.

questions.question: What is the judgment called which we have to undergo immediately after death?
questions.answer: The judgment we have to undergo immediately after death is called the Particular Judgment.

questions.question: What is the judgment called which all men have to undergo on the last day?
questions.answer: The judgment which all men have to undergo on the last day is called the General Judgment.

questions.question: What is heaven?
questions.answer: Heaven is the state of everlasting life in which we see God face to face, are made like to Him in glory, and enjoy eternal happiness.

questions.question: What is purgatory?
questions.answer: Purgatory is the state in which those suffer for a time who die guilty of venial sins, or without having fully satisfied for the punishment due to their sins.

questions.question: Can the faithful on earth help the souls in purgatory?
questions.answer: The faithful on earth can help the souls in purgatory by their prayers, fasting, almsgiving, and by having Masses said for them.

questions.question: What is hell?
questions.answer: Hell is a state of everlasting punishment in which the wicked suffer in both soul and body.

questions.question: Will the punishment of the wicked last forever?
questions.answer: The punishment of the wicked will last forever.

questions.question: What are the chief punishments of hell?
questions.answer: The chief punishments of hell are the eternal separation from God and everlasting fire.

questions.question: What are sacramentals?
questions.answer: Sacramentals are holy things or actions of which the Church makes use to obtain for us from God, through her intercession, spiritual and temporal favors.

questions.question: What is the difference between sacraments and sacramentals?
questions.answer: The difference between sacraments and sacramentals is: first, the sacraments were instituted by Jesus Christ and the sacramentals were instituted by the Church; second, the sacraments give grace of themselves when we place no obstacle in the way; the sacramentals excite in us pious dispositions, by means of which we may obtain grace.

questions.question: What are the chief kinds of sacramentals?
questions.answer: The chief kinds of sacramentals are blessings given by priests and bishops, exorcisms against evil spirits, blessed objects of devotion.

questions.question: What is the Sign of the Cross?
questions.answer: The Sign of the Cross is a sacramental that reminds us of the Blessed Trinity and of our redemption by the death of Jesus Christ on the cross.

questions.question: What is holy water?
questions.answer: Holy water is water blessed by the priest with solemn prayer to beg God's blessing on those who use it and protection from the powers of darkness.

questions.question: What does faith oblige us to believe?
questions.answer: Faith obliges us to believe all the truths which the Holy Catholic Church teaches.

questions.question: Can faith alone save us?
questions.answer: Faith alone cannot save us without good works; we must also have hope and charity.

questions.question: What are the Beatitudes?
questions.answer: The Beatitudes are the teachings of our Lord in the Sermon on the Mount which describe the qualities of those who shall be blessed and rewarded in heaven.

questions.question: Repeat the Beatitudes.
questions.answer: 1. Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven. 2. Blessed are the meek, for they shall possess the earth. 3. Blessed are they that mourn, for they shall be comforted. 4. Blessed are they that hunger and thirst after justice, for they shall be filled. 5. Blessed are the merciful, for they shall obtain mercy. 6. Blessed are the clean of heart, for they shall see God. 7. Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called children of God. 8. Blessed are they that suffer persecution for justice' sake, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.

questions.question: What are the corporal works of mercy?
questions.answer: The corporal works of mercy are seven: To feed the hungry, to give drink to the thirsty, to clothe the naked, to shelter the homeless, to visit the sick, to visit the imprisoned, and to bury the dead.

questions.question: What are the spiritual works of mercy?
questions.answer: The spiritual works of mercy are seven: To admonish the sinner, to instruct the ignorant, to counsel the doubtful, to comfort the sorrowful, to bear wrongs patiently, to forgive all injuries, and to pray for the living and the dead.

questions.question: What are the fruits of the Holy Ghost?
questions.answer: The fruits of the Holy Ghost are charity, joy, peace, patience, benignity, goodness, long-suffering, mildness, faith, modesty, continency, and chastity.

questions.question: What are the sins against the Holy Ghost?
questions.answer: The sins against the Holy Ghost are six: Presumption of God's mercy, despair, impugning the known truth, envy at another's spiritual good, obstinacy in sin, and final impenitence.

questions.question: What are the four sins crying to heaven for vengeance?
questions.answer: The four sins crying to heaven for vengeance are willful murder, the sin of Sodom, oppression of the poor, and defrauding laborers of their wages.
